sections.content:
This document serves as an interface description for the transfer of orders for production at
allcop. In particular, it describes the basic structure of an order, as well as the transmission via
FTPS and SFTP, standards that the files must satisfy and the structure of the order XML.
In the last part, it is explained, in which manner status messages of the production system are
sent and which alternatives are possible.
This document or parts of this document may not be distributed or used for other purposes
than for use at allcop without the consent of allcop.

Each order consists of an order directory and, depending on the order type, the image files in
JPG or PDF format, invoice files in zipped PS format and the order XML. (see fig. 1.)

(Fig. 1.: Example order)

2.1.1 Order directory
The name of the order directory is created as follows:
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa
Characters Description
JJJJMMTThhmmss Date-time of order (year-month-day-hour-minute-second)
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)
Seite 3 von 24 02.10.2025 DS
2.1.2 Order-XML
The order-XML is in XML format. The structure of the file name is described below.
odppppaaaaaaaa.xml
Characters Description
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)

2.1.3 Image files
The file name structure for image files is described as follows.
bdppppaaaaaaaa-nnnn.jpg
bfppppaaaaaaaa-nnnn.pdf
cfppppaaaaaaaa-nnnn.pdf
Characters Description
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)
nnnn Sequential number of the image file with leading 0 (starts at 0)

2.1.4 Document files
Should our production system print invoice/delivery notes or other documents, they must be
in PostScript format in zipped format. If ZIP-compression is not possible, the file extension
needs to be ps.zip in any case.
A maximum of 2 files are possible.
The file name structure for the document files is described as follows.
ddppppaaaaaaaa-nnnn.ps.zip
Characters Description
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)
nnnn Sequential number of the document file with leading 0 (starts at 0)

allcop supports the following protocols for the transmission of orders from the customer:
FTPS and SFTP.
Since personal customer data is subject to confidentiality, the use of the encrypted protocols
FTPS and SFTP is mandatory.
The customer provides an FTP server from which allcop collects the orders by means of
download. This way, the lossless transmission of orders can be guaranteed should the
Seite 4 von 24 02.10.2025 DS
internet connection fail. The same server is used to upload messages relating to the
production status of an order.

• Write permission is required in the directories
• There should be no limit to the number of simultaneous connections.

heading_path: 3.2. Directory structure of the FTP server
sections.content:
(Abb. 2.: Directory structure)

Starting from a root directory (optionally root (/), or another directory) there must exist 3
folders with write permission. In the example above, (see fig. 2) these are named send, recv,
done. The customer can choose any name, but the function of these folders always remains
the same.
Directory Description
send Directory in which the order directories including contents are
provided.
recv Directory to which the order status updates are uploaded to seperate
order directories.
done Directory to which the order directories are moved after successful
download.

The download of an order by allcop is trigger-driven. This means that an order directory is
downloaded only after the order directory is complete and the trigger file is present in the
order directory. This trigger file can be the order-XML or an arbitrary 0-byte file. As long as
Seite 5 von 24 02.10.2025 DS
this file is not present, any order files can be uploaded in any time frame. It is important that
once present, a file name cannot be changed afterwards. Each file is registered and a check
for integrity is performed after the download.
In case the customer uses temporary file names, it is recommended to upload the order
outside the scope of the “send” directory and move it to the “send” directory after
completion with a “move” command (See fig. 3). This process is atomic.

(Abb. 3.: Order directory outside scope of „send“-directory)
After successful download the order is moved to the “done” folder. This procedure is
performed once. If the there is already a folder of the same name in the “done” folder, the
process will fail. The order will however, still be produced and no new download will be
performed for the entire production process and archiving period. The archiving period starts
with the registration of an order directory. Only after this period expires, the order is deleted
from the database and is registered as a new order. Therefore it is the customers
responsibility to clean the “done” directory on a regular basis.
Seite 6 von 24 02.10.2025 DS

The XML file corresponds to XML version 1.0 and must be encoded in UTF-8. It has the
following structure:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Order version="2.1">
<OrderData />
<DealerAdresse />
<KundenAdresse />
<LieferAdresse />
<PickPoint />
<Rechnungsdaten />
<JPGs count="0" />
<PureFotoBook count="0" />
 <PickProducts count="0" />
</Order>

The individual order XML nodes are described below.
Seite 7 von 24 02.10.2025 DS

4.3.1 Special case Packstation
By specifying the node Packstation with the attribute used=”1”, the information contained
within the node Packstation will be implemented for DHLs routing coding. Any information
contained within Strasse and Hausnummer will be ignored.

Node structure if products (pure photo books, calendars, folding cards) are provided in
PDF format (pre processed):

<Template FileName="EP_6_hoch.png" />

</Product>
</PureFotoBook>

Node structure if digital print books are provided:
The products (books) are supplied in two PDF files, the book contents and the cover. These
products receive the type “P”. The cover files must be supplied with the fixed identifier “cf”.

<Product Artikel="17030000" Typ=”P” Kopien="2" Preis="0032000">
<BookFile FileName="bf945512020569-0001.pdf" />
<CoverFile FileName="cf945512020569-0001.pdf" />
<Enhance>1</Enhance>
</Product>
<Product Artikel="17030000" Typ=”P” Kopien="3" Preis="0032000">
<BookFile FileName="bf945512020569-0002.pdf" />
<CoverFile FileName="cf945512020569-0002.pdf" />
<Enhance>0</Enhance>
</Product>
</PureFotoBook>

After the order has been transferred to the allcop production system, allcop creates a
subdirectory with the name of the order on the FTP server in the feedback directory provided
for this purpose.
The status messages of the order are uploaded to this subdirectory as files. The feedback
messages always refer to the entire order. Status confirmation at item level is not supported.
(See figure 4).
The portal operator is responsible for processing these status messages and eventual
dispatching of e-mails to the end customer.
In the event you have been assigned the Portal-ID 2000, the files will be deposited directly
into the “recv”-directory as described under section 5.3 and not in subdirectories.
Seite 22 von 24 02.10.2025 DS

(Fig. 4.: Complete set of order status files)

The directory name of an order is provided in allcop’s format:
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa
Characters Description
JJJJMMTThhmmss Date-time of order (year-month-day-hour-minute-second)
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)

The order status files sent by allcop are transferred in the exact moment the order passes the
corresponding production status:
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa_JJJJMMTThhmmss_TRANSFERRED
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa_JJJJMMTThhmmss_IMPORTED
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa_JJJJMMTThhmmss_MANUFACTURED
JJJJMMTThhmmss_ppppaaaaaaaa_JJJJMMTThhmmss_SHIPPED

Characters Description
JJJJMMTThhmmss Date-time of order (year-month-day-hour-minute-second)
JJJJMMTThhmmss Timestamp of the update (year-month-day-hour-minute-second)
pppp 4-digit portal id (issued by allcop)
aaaaaaaa Sequential 8-digit order id with leading 0 (issued by the customer)
TRANSFERRED The order was successfully transferred to the production system
Seite 23 von 24 02.10.2025 DS
IMPORTED The order was imported into the production system. Production will
now commence.
MANUFACTURED The production of the order was successfully completed
SHIPPED The order has just been shipped.

Depending on customer requirements, either all order status files can be transferred or just
the SHIPPED file.

Die TRANSFERRED file is always empty.
The IMPORTED file is identical to the original order XML
The MANUFACTURED file consists of up to two lines.
The first line contains the internal customer number which is associated with the order.
The second line optionally contains the used postage product number.
The SHIPPED file is identical to the original order XML, however contains the tracking
information as additional tags within the node OrderData.
Example:
<OrderData>
<PortalID>9527</PortalID>
<wwwNr>E9VNFK4</wwwNr>
<Orderdate>20161127193941</Orderdate>
<Eingang>20161127204343</Eingang>
<OrderID>42870653</OrderID>
<OrderUuid>dc7bf559-6e7b-49fc-b5d9-cd8f5030c357</OrderUuid>
<DealerID>9527</DealerID>
<Endkunde/>
<Waehrung/>
<Versandart>HOME</Versandart>
<Anlieferung/>
<Bestellweg/>

Bei den Hardcover-Digitaldruck-Fotobüchern handelt es sich um Fotobücher die die
im Digitaldruck gedruckt werden. Der Kunde hat die Möglichkeit sowohl das Cover
als auch die Seiten vollständig individuell zu gestalten.
Hardcover digital print photo books are those that are printed in a digital format. The
customer has the option of designing both the cover and the pages completely
individually.

Im Folgenden werden die Anforderungen an die PDF-Datei beschrieben, wie sie für
die Produktion der Digitaldruck-Fotobücher von allcop Farbbildservice GmbH & Co.
KG benötigt wird.
The requirements for the PDF file for hardcover production from allcop Farbbildservice
GmbH & Co KG. are described as follows:
Seite 3 von 12 24.09.2025 DM

heading_path: 4. Anforderungen an die PDF -Datei / PDF file requirements
sections.content:
Für ein Digitaldruck-Fotobuch sind 2 PDF-Dateien notwendig. Eine Cover-PDF und
eine Seiten-PDF. Die Cover PDF enthält eine Seite mit dem Cover, während es sich bei
der Seiten-PDF um eine mehrseitige PDF-Datei handelt, die alle Seiten des Buches
von 1 bis x enthält. Die Maße des Covers und der Seiten müssen den im Folgenden
aufgeführten Maßen entsprechen.
Weitere generelle Anforderungen an die PDF-Datei:
 Full-Flated
 Farbraum: sRGB
 Auflösung: 300 dpi

2 PDF files are required for a digital print photobook. One PDF for the cover and one for
the pages. The cover PDF contains one page with the cover, while the page PDF is a
multi-page PDF file containing all pages of the book from 1 to x. The dimensions of the
cover and pages must correspond to the dimensions listed below.
Additional general requirements for the PDF file:
 Full-flated
 Colour spectrum: sRGB
 Resolution: 300 dpi

4.1 Aufbau des Covers / Structure of the cover
Der Aufbau des Covers ist in Fehler! Verweisquelle konnte nicht gefunden
werden. dargestellt. In den Tabellen sind die tatsächlichen Werte aufgeführt. Es ist
jedoch zu berücksichtigen, dass es zu Abweichungen durch Produktionstoleranzen
bei Beschnittkanten, Einschlagskanten, Falzkanten kommen kann.
The structure of the cover is illustrated in Abbildung 4.1 – 1 / Diagram 4.1 - 1. The tables
show the actual values. There may, however, be deviations due to production tolerances
for trimmed edges, wrapping edges and folded edges which need to be taken into
account.
Seite 4 von 12 24.09.2025 DM

Abbildung 4.1 – 1 / Diagram 4.1 - 1

Für die PDF sind die Maße B (Gesamtbreite des Covers) und H (Gesamthöhe des
Covers) von Bedeutung. Diesen Maßen muss das Cover in der PDF-Datei entsprechen.
The PDF is based on the dimensions B (total width of the cover) and H (total height of
the cover). The cover in the PDF file must correspond to these dimensions.
Seite 5 von 12 24.09.2025 DM
Bei den Werten T1 (Einschlag rechts/links) und T2 (Einschlag oben/unten) handelt es
sich um die Bereiche des Covers, die bei der Cover-Produktion nach innen
umgeschlagen werden und damit auf der Buchvorderseite nicht mehr sichtbar sind.
Damit ergibt sich für den sichtbaren Bereich des Covers beim fertigen Buch eine
effektive Höhe von HS und eine effektive Breite BS die sich aus D+E+F+E+D
zusammensetzt.
The values T1 (right/left margin) and T2 (top/bottom margin) are the areas of the cover
that are folded inwards during cover production and therefore no longer visible on the
front page of the book. This results in an effective height of HS and an effective width
BS consisting of D+E+F+E+D for the visible area of the cover of the finished book.

Die maximale Schriftgröße auf dem Buchrücken ist von dessen Breite abhängig. Um
hier Probleme zu vermeiden darf die maximale Schrifthöhe inklusive Ober- und
Unterlängen 2 mm weniger als die Buchrückenbreite betragen. Die Orientierung
sollte mittig angelegt werden.
The maximum font size on the spine depends on its width. The maximum font height
including ascenders and descenders is 2 mm less than the spine width in order to avoid
potential problems. The alignment should be in the middle.

Der aus produktionstechnischen Gründen notwendige Barcode befindet sich auf der
Rückseite des Covers. Die Lage und Größe des Feldes ist in Abbildung 2.1-1
wiedergegeben. Das Barcodefeld sollte in der Bestellsoftware, in der angegebenen
Größe visualisiert werden. Der Barcode selbst wird vom Labor erzeugt und in einem
weißen Feld aufgebracht.
Für jedes Buchformat wird eine jpg-Datei bereitgestellt, mit der die dargestellten
Maße im Client überprüft werden können
.
The barcode required for production reasons is shown on the back of the cover. The
position and size of the field is shown in Diagram 2.1-1. The barcode field should be
depicted in the software in the specified size when ordering. The barcode itself is
generated by the laboratory and applied in a white field.
A jpg file is provided for each book format, allowing the client to check the dimensions
displayed.

In den folgenden Tabellen sind die Werte für die einzelnen Parameter aufgeführt.
The following tables show the values for the individual parameters.

4.2 Aufbau der Innenseiten / Structure of the inner pages
In Abbildung 4.2 - 1 ist der Aufbau der linken und der rechten Seite dargestellt.
Diagram 4.2-1 shows the structure of a double book page.


Abbildung 4.2 – 1 / Diagram 4.2 -1

4.2.1 Anlieferung der Buchseiten / Delivery of the book pages
Die Seiten werden immer als Einzelseiten angeliefert. Für die PDF sind die Maße B
(Gesamtbreite einer Bucheinzelseite) und H (Gesamthöhe einer Bucheinzelseite) von
Bedeutung. Diesen Maßen muss eine Bucheinzelseite in der PDF entsprechen.
Pages are always delivered as individual pages. The dimensions B (total width of an
individual book page) and H (total height of an individual book page) have specific
relevance for the PDF. An individual book page in the PDF must correspond to these
dimensions.
Seite 8 von 12 24.09.2025 DM
Der produktionsbedingte Beschnitt beträgt T2 (Beschnitt außen), T3 (Beschnitt innen)
und T1 (Beschnitt oben/unten). Daraus ergibt sich der sichtbare Bereich HS (Höhe
einer Buchseite) und BS (Breite einer Buchseite).
The production-related trimming is T2 (outer trim), T3 (inner trim) and T1 (top/bottom
trim). This results in the visible area HS (height of a book page) and BS (width of a book
page).

heading_path: 5. Anforderungen an die XML -Datei / Requirements for the
sections.content:
XML file
Damit eine korrekte Verarbeitung der Daten erfolgen kann, muss die 8-stellige
Produktionsnummer, die Bezeichnung der Coverdatei (CoverFile FileName) und
Seitendatei (BookFile FileName), in der XML-Datei mit übergeben werden.
In order for the data to be processed correctly, the 8-digit production number, the name
of the cover file (CoverFile FileName) and page file (BookFile FileName) must also be
transferred in the XML file.
Beispiel Auszug aus der XML: / Example excerpt from the XML:
<PureFotoBook count="1">
 <Product Artikel="80130000" Typ="P" Kopien="1" Preis="0050050">
 <BookFile FileName="bf403110329876-0000.pdf" />
 <CoverFile FileName="cf403110329876-0000.pdf" />
 <Enhance>0</Enhance>
 </Product>
</PureFotoBook>
Seite 9 von 12 24.09.2025 DM
